title: Which Will Smith Character Are You Actually Living Like Right Now?
dek: From Bel-Air swagger to the last man on Earth — your answers will expose exactly which Will Smith role you've secretly been cosplaying as this whole time.

quiz.intro: No wrong answers here — just answer like nobody's watching and we'll tell you which Will Smith icon you truly are.

questions.question: It's Friday night. What's actually happening?

answers.text: Chasing something weird across the city with a partner I didn't sign up for.
Standing in front of a mirror rehearsing my next big statement to the world.
Working a side hustle until sunrise — sleep is for people with savings.
Staying in, running the numbers, talking to my dog like he understands me.
I said I was the greatest before I even proved it.
Nobody's ever going to tell me I can't do something.
Keep the routine. The routine keeps you alive.
There's always a chance — you just have to be prepared for it.

questions.question: Someone tries to embarrass you in front of a crowd. Your move?

answers.text: Brush it off — I don't have time, I've got bigger fights.
Barely register it. I talk to mannequins more than people anyway.
Charm them until they forget they were ever mad.
Get right up in their face. Fear isn't really in my vocabulary.
Being the very last one left.
Getting caught actually being vulnerable.
Losing my partner out in the field.
Another ten thousand years with no one to talk to.
Perfectly tailored. Not a single wrinkle allowed.
Leather jacket, aviators, watch that costs more than your car.
Shapeshifting into whatever the bit calls for.
Tactical gear, red scope, zero exceptions.

questions.question: How would your best friend describe you?

answers.text: "Reckless — but somehow he's always right."
"The funniest person alive who also gives the best advice."
"Terrifying at work, soft as anything with the people he loves."
"The one who somehow figures it out at the very last second."

questions.question: What are you ordering at the bar?

answers.text: Whatever's biggest, with a straw and some theatrics.
Whiskey, neat. Not looking for small talk.
Something strong — trying to forget the last mind-wipe.
Nothing. I'm in training.

questions.question: Pick a superpower you'd actually want.

answers.text: Perfect aim. Every shot, no exceptions.
A memory-erasing gadget. Obviously.
The ability to make anyone believe in themselves the way I believe in me.
More hours in the day. That's it. That's the whole wish.

questions.question: How do you handle a breakup?

answers.text: Crack jokes until it stops hurting, then get back to work.
Turn it into fuel and prove everyone wrong.
No time to dwell — the kid needs me more than I need to feel sad.
Retreat even further inward. Talk to the walls instead.
Somewhere with cameras and a crowd waiting.
Can't afford one — but I'd take my kid anywhere free.
Nowhere. I don't leave the safe zone.
A city built for romance, itinerary included.

results.name: Agent J
results.subtitle: Men in Black (1997)
results.description: You're the wisecracking new guy who somehow figures it out faster than everyone who's been doing this for years. Once an NYPD street cop, J got scouted mid-chase and thrown into a world of aliens with nothing but attitude and instinct to guide him — and honestly, it worked out fine. You bring chaos and humor to every uptight room you walk into, and people secretly love you for it.

results.name: Muhammad Ali
results.subtitle: Ali (2001)
results.description: You talk a big game because you can absolutely back it up. Smith spent months training and studying Ali's cadence to capture a man who took on the U.S. government and refused to back down from his convictions, even when it cost him his title and prime years. You'd rather lose everything standing on principle than win by staying quiet.

results.name: Chris Gardner
results.subtitle: The Pursuit of Happyness (2006)
results.description: You are relentless, full stop. Chris Gardner spent nearly a year homeless with his young son while chasing an unpaid stockbroker internship that had a one-in-twenty shot of paying off — and he never once let anyone see him sweat. If there's a door even slightly open, you're already walking through it, kid on your hip and all.

results.name: Robert Neville
results.subtitle: I Am Legend (2007)
results.description: You're disciplined to a fault and fiercely self-reliant, even when it's clearly taking a toll. Neville spent three years as New York's last known survivor, running the same rigid routine, talking to mannequins, and refusing to give up on a cure long after hope should've run out. You keep it together because someone has to, even if that someone is just you.

results.name: Alex "Hitch" Hitchens
results.subtitle: Hitch (2005)
results.description: You've got a plan for everyone else's love life and zero clue what to do with your own. Hitch built an entire secret career as New York's legendary "Date Doctor," guaranteed to get any guy to a third date — right up until his own charm completely failed him with the one person who mattered. Smooth in public, a mess in private. Relatable, honestly.

results.name: Mike Lowrey
results.subtitle: Bad Boys (1995)
results.description: You're loud, a little reckless, and somehow always right in the end. Mike is a wealthy, sharp-dressed Miami detective who could've coasted on his family money but works harder than anyone in the department to prove he earned his badge. You're in it for the thrill as much as the job, and everyone around you knows it.

results.name: Genie
results.subtitle: Aladdin (2019)
results.description: You're the life of every party and the one giving the realest advice in the room, often at the same time. Genie granted Aladdin's wishes for ten thousand years without ever once calling a master a friend — until Aladdin broke that streak. You'll bend the rules for the people you love, even when it costs you.

results.name: Deadshot
results.subtitle: Suicide Squad (2016)
results.description: You look terrifying from the outside, but you'd tear the world apart for the one person who actually matters to you. Deadshot is the deadliest marksman in the DC universe who's never missed a shot, yet his real motivation was always earning enough time off his sentence to be with his daughter Zoe again. Cold exterior, soft core — don't let anyone tell you otherwise.

sources.title: Deadshot | DC Extended Universe Wiki | Fandom
